coredata.dc:title: Research on battery SOH estimation algorithm of energy storage frequency modulation system

coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Xiwen
Li, Jia
Yao, Zhuohong
Wang, Zhongyan
Si, Ruicai
Diao, Yunpeng
coredata.dc:description: Based on the experimental data, the life characteristics of lithium-ion battery were analyzed. According to the experimental data statistics, the battery life changes with the number of cycles, the performance of the battery after repeated use is studied, and the changes of battery capacity, discharge efficiency, energy efficiency, internal resistance and other parameters with the battery life are explored. The mathematical model of battery life and battery usage times is established by curve fitting, and the SOH of battery is predicted according to this model. Finally, the SOH of Li ion battery is estimated according to the voltage curve fitting method. Firstly, the battery charging curves under different discharge rates and different SOH are normalized, and the charging voltage error after normalization calculation is compared. According to the existing experimental data, the SOH estimation algorithm of 92Ah lithium-ion battery is verified, the estimation accuracy of voltage curve fitting method is verified, and the estimation results of SOH are analyzed.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium battery
SOH
Energy storage
Curve fitting

The area and error of the normalized curve.

ce:dochead.ce:textfn: 2021 6th International Conference on Clean Energy and Power Generation Technology (CEPGT 2021), September 10–12, 2021, Shanghai, China

head.ce:title: Research on battery SOH estimation algorithm of energy storage frequency modulation system

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Jilin Province Electric Power Science Research Institute, No. 4433 Renmin Street, Changchun, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Jilin Province Electric Power Science Research Institute

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Grid Jilin Electric Power Co., Ltd, No. 4629 Renmin Street Changchun, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Grid Jilin Electric Power Co., Ltd

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Grid Jilin Electric Power Co.,Ltd, No.4629 Renmin Street Changchun, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Based on the experimental data, the life characteristics of lithium-ion battery were analyzed. According to the experimental data statistics, the battery life changes with the number of cycles, the performance of the battery after repeated use is studied, and the changes of battery capacity, discharge efficiency, energy efficiency, internal resistance and other parameters with the battery life are explored. The mathematical model of battery life and battery usage times is established by curve fitting, and the SOH of battery is predicted according to this model. Finally, the SOH of Li ion battery is estimated according to the voltage curve fitting method. Firstly, the battery charging curves under different discharge rates and different SOH are normalized, and the charging voltage error after normalization calculation is compared. According to the existing experimental data, the SOH estimation algorithm of 92Ah lithium-ion battery is verified, the estimation accuracy of voltage curve fitting method is verified, and the estimation results of SOH are analyzed.

ce:section.ce:para: The energy storage technology has become a key method for power grid with the increasing capacity of new energy power plants in recent years [1]. The installed capacity of new energy storage projects in China was 2.3 GW in 2018. The new capacity of electrochemical energy storage was 0.6 GW which grew 414% year on year [2]. By the end of the fourteenth five year plan the installed capacity of energy storage in China will reach 50–60 GW and by 2050 it will reach more than 200 GW. The market size will exceed 2 trillion yuan according to this calculation [3].

It is worth noting that the capacity of the battery will decay after a long time of use. The performance of the battery will be severely reduced. Overcharging and overdischarging will occur when the battery is charged and discharged if the remaining battery capacity is unknown. This will aggravate the aging of the battery and increase the cost of electrochemical energy storage in disguise. Therefore, it is very important to accurately estimate battery performance and predict the remaining life of the battery [4].

We use SOH (State of Health) to indicate the health of the battery. The battery should be replaced when the capacity of fully charged battery is less than 80% of the battery’s rated capacity according to the IEEE 1188-1996 standard.

According to this standard the SOH can be defined as the ratio of the discharge capacity of a battery to the rated capacity of a new battery under a certain condition [5]:
(1)
SOH =
Q
n o w
Q
n e w
× 100 %

This paper includes the following parts. The battery used in this paper is lithium iron phosphate battery. The capacity of the battery is 92 Ah. We analysis the life characteristics of lithium-ion battery based on the experimental data. We explore the law of battery capacity, discharge efficiency, energy efficiency, internal resistance and other parameters with battery life. We use curve fitting to establish a mathematical model of battery life and estimate the SOH of the battery based on this model.
The batteries used in this paper are lithium iron phosphate battery which are applied to an energy storage power station project. The capacity of energy storage power station is 10 MWh.

The energy storage power station is composed of 19008 batteries. Each 24 batteries form a battery module and every 12 battery modules form a battery cluster. The battery capacity is 92 Ah and the energy is 294.4 Wh. The composition of the battery is shown in Fig. 1.
In this paper we test the single lithium-ion battery. We use the charge–discharge cycle test to test the battery life. The steps of battery cycle life test are as follows [6]:

1. Calibrate the battery capacity. Discharge the battery at a constant current with a C/3 current. Stop discharging when the voltage is close to 3 V. Repeat the experiment for several times. We considered the measured capacity to be the actual capacity of the battery if the discharge capacity error does not exceed 2% each time.
2. Charge the battery with the standard charging methods.
3. Put the battery aside for 12 h until the battery performance is stable.

4. Discharge with 1 C current to the cut-off voltage (3 V) of the battery and put it aside for 20 min to stabilize the performance of the battery. Then discharge with 0.2 C current to the cut-off voltage (3 V) and put it aside for 20 min.

5. Charge with 1 C current to the cut-off voltage (4.2 V) of the battery and put it aside for 20 min to stabilize the battery performance. Then charge with 0.2 C current to the cut-off voltage (4.2 V) and put it aside for 20 min.
6. Repeat steps 4 to 5 of the experiment 50 times.
7. Calibrate the battery capacity.
8. Repeat steps 3 to 7 until the SOH of the battery is 80% which is regarded as the end of battery life.
The flowchart of the battery life experiment is shown in Fig. 2.

The experiment was repeated 450 times in total. The life experiment data is shown in Table 1. The battery will be aged after being used many times. This is because it occurs overreacts chemical reaction inside the battery which decreases the performance of the battery. The structure of the battery’s positive and negative electrodes is deformed.
We can draw the change curve of battery available capacity and Coulomb efficiency according to the data in Table 1, as shown in Figs. 3 and 4.

The available capacity of the battery have obvious changes as the number of charge and discharge cycles increases. When the cycle time is about 50 times, the available capacity of the battery reaches the maximum capacity. The battery performance changes obviously when the cycle time is close to 200 times. The available capacity begin to decrease due to the irreversible chemical reaction inside the battery.

We establish the battery life model based on the data given in Table 1. We use the trend of available capacity with the increase of cycle times to express the change of SOH. We give the mathematical relationship between the SOH and the cycle times according to Fig. 3. The curve comparison is shown in Fig. 5.

The curve fitting has the highest accuracy with the 8th-order polynomial after calculation. The curve fitting formula is as follows.
(2)
SOH = f
x , t
The coefficients of polynomial are shown in Table 2.
It can be seen from Fig. 5 that the relationship between SOH and cycle time has a good fit. But the applicability of this method is poor.

It is necessary to re-check the SOH and cycle number of the battery when the battery type changes. That will take a lot of time. In order to improve the versatility of SOH estimation and save time, the SOH estimation method needs to be adjusted.

ce:section.ce:section-title: SOH estimation based on voltage curve fitting
ce:section.ce:para: When a large current flows through the battery, the voltage changes very significantly for an aging battery. The voltage curve fitting to estimate current SOH of the battery is based on the characteristic that the changing value of voltage is different under different SOH [7]. This paper selects the voltage curve fitting to estimate the SOH of the battery. The method is shown in Fig. 6.

Considering the discharging conditions of batteries are irregular and the charging is regular. We use the charging voltage curve to estimate SOH when using the voltage curve fitting in this paper. We normalize the charge voltage curves of batteries under different SOH and integrate into one coordinate system for comparison. Then we can estimate the SOH of the battery based on a charge curve

The voltage curves collected are very similar when charging batteries under constant current conditions. But the charging time of these curves is different. This paper uses the following formula to normalize these curves.
(3)
V
u
=
V −
V
e
/
V
p
−
V
e
(4)
T
w
=
C
e
/ 92 ∗ 3600
(5)
T
u
=
T −
T
e
/
T
p
−
T
e
+
T
w

After normalization calculation, the coordinates of the charging voltage and charging time during the battery charging process are both constrained between 0 and 1. In order to verify the consistency of the normalized calculation of the battery charging voltage curve under different SOH, this paper adopts the method of linear interpolation to compare the area of the normalized curve. The formula is shown as follow.

Use italics for variables (u) and bold (u) for vectors. The order for brackets should be {[()]}, except where brackets have special significance.
(6)
S =
∑
n = 2
n = N
V
u
( n )
+
V
u
( n − 1 )
/ 2
t
u
( n )
−
t
u
( n − 1 )

V
u
is normalized voltage and
T
u
is normalized charging time.

An important problem of using the voltage curve fitting to estimate SOH is that the normalized curve must have good consistency under different SOH. This paper select the 50th, 100th, 200th, 300th and 400th cycle life experiments to normalize the charging voltage curve. We can see the curve Fig. 7.

It can be seen from Fig. 7that the four charging curves under different SOH have good consistency except the 400th charging curve. We calculate the area and error under different SOH by calculating the area of the normalized curve. The results are shown in Table 3.

We choose the 50th charging curve as the basic curve. As the number of cycles increases, the calculation error of the normalized curve will increase. This will affect the accuracy of SOH estimation. The error is the smallest when the number of battery cycles is close to 50 times and the SOH of the battery can be estimated accurately. The error of this method is less than 5% within 300 times. The error increases significantly when more than 300 times. The calculated area error is more than 10% at 400 times.

The SOH means the capacity of a battery to store power. The basic idea of estimating SOH is charging the same amount of power in the battery within a certain time and the voltage changes differently at different SOH.
(7)
SOH =
t
2
−
t
1
I
c
2
−
c
1
C

We can calculate the SOH according to (7). When the battery is charging, the battery capacities at time
t
1
and time
t
2
are
c
1
and
c
2
. I is the charging current of the battery and C is the battery rated capacity.

We can use
t
2
−
t
1
as the charging time of the battery. We define
c
1
as the beginning of charging so
c
1
is zero. The charging current I and the rated capacity C of the battery are both fixed values. So the estimation of SOH is only related to the charging time t and the normalized capacity
c
2
. We take the 100th and 400th charging voltages as examples, the relationship between the estimated SOH and the normalized charging voltage is shown in Figs. 8 and 9.

It can be seen that the SOH of the battery oscillates sharply at the initial time of estimation. This is because the battery starts to charge when the SOC is zero. The electrochemical performance of the battery is not stable. The performance of the battery stabilizes as the charging process progresses and the SOH estimation result will stabilize. Table 4 shows the estimated results of battery SOH.

It can be seen from Table 4 that the estimation error of SOH is relatively large between the 200th and 250th times and the maximum error is 5.03%. The estimation error of SOH near the 200th cycle is the largest. The estimation error of SOH by voltage curve fitting is less than 6%.

A lot of experiments have been done in this paper. The SOH of battery was estimated by voltage curve fitting. We analyze the experimental data we get and study the variation of battery SOH. It can be known that the estimation accuracy of SOH is relatively accurate and the maximum estimation error is less than 7%.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This paper is funded by the science project of State Grid of Jilinsheng Electric Power Co., Ltd. [Grant-number: KY-GS-21-01-04; KY-GS-20-01-02].

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Electrochemical mechanical coupled modeling and parameterization of swelling and ionic transport in lithium-ion batteries

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Sauerteig D, Hanselmann N, Arzberger A, Electrochemical mechanical coupled modeling and parameterization of swelling and ionic transport in lithium-ion batteries[J]. Journal of Power Sources, 2018, 378: 235-247.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Data-driven hybrid remaining useful life estimation approach for spacecraft lithium-ion battery

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Song Yuchen, Liu Datong, Yang Chen, Data-driven hybrid remaining useful life estimation approach for spacecraft lithium-ion battery[J]. Microelectronics Reliability, 2017, 75(6): 142-153.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Algorithms for advanced battery management systems

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Nalin A. Reinhardt, Jake, Algorithms for advanced Battery Management Systems[J]. IEEE Control Systems Magazine, 2010, 30, 3: 49-68.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Investigations of lithium-sulfur batteries using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Natalia A, Can, Kei Hirose, Investigations of lithium-sulfur batteries using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy[J]. Electrochimica Acta, 2013, 97: 42-51.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Theory of battery aging in a lithium-ion battery: Capacity fade, nonlinear ageing and lifetime prediction

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Atalay S, Sheikh M, Mariani A. Theory of battery aging in a lithium-ion battery: Capacity fade, nonlinear ageing and lifetime prediction[J]. Journal of Power Sources, 2020, 478: 229026-229034.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Effects of external pressure on the performance and ageing of single-layer lithium-ion pouch cells

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Mussa A S, Klett M, Lindbergh G. Effects of external pressure on the performance and ageing of single-layer lithium-ion pouch cells[J]. Journal of Power Sources, 2018, 385: 18-26.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Robust prognostics for state of health estimation of lithium-ion batteries based on an improved PSO-SVR model

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Qin Taichun, Zeng Shengkui, Guo Jianbin, Robust prognostics for state of health estimation of lithium-ion batteries based on an improved PSO-SVR model[J]. Microelectronics Reliability, 2015, 55(9-10): 1280-1284.
